 Build to Last

We design for safety and reliability so clinicians, patients, and our teams can trust what we build every day.

About the job
About Zero to Claw
Zero to Claw is a white-glove OpenClaw setup and automation service. We deploy security-hardened, production-ready OpenClaw AI agents for businesses. Our clients range from solo operators who want a tireless digital assistant to small teams who need agents handling email triage, meeting prep, CRM workflows, and cross-app automation.
Our founders bring deep experience: Jake has led $100M+ in business transformation, and Daniel is a former Atlassian Engineer with 6+ years shipping Jira, Bitbucket, and Confluence to millions of users.

The Role
We need someone to help us deliver custom OpenClaw workflows for our clients and maintain their running instances. You'll be the person who takes a client's messy "I want my AI to do X when Y happens" request, figures out how to wire it up in OpenClaw, builds it, tests it, and makes sure it keeps running. You'll also handle troubleshooting when things break — a misconfigured MCP server, a flaky WhatsApp integration, a gateway auth issue, an agent that's burning through API credits. You'll be deploying, configuring, integrating, and debugging real systems for real clients on real deadlines.

What you'll do
Connect OpenClaw to client tools — Gmail, Notion, Slack, Jira, Salesforce, databases, and hundreds more
Build custom workflows and skills when off-the-shelf integrations don't cut it
Troubleshoot live client instances — broken integrations, runaway API costs, flaky messaging channels, agent misbehaviour
Talk to clients, understand what they need, and translate that into working automation

What will help you succeed
Comfort in a terminal — SSH, config files, logs, basic server admin
Some experience with any programming language — enough to read code, write scripts, and hit APIs
Basic understanding of networking — ports, firewalls, DNS
Familiarity with Docker
Clear communication — you'll explain technical things to non-technical people regularly
A bias toward figuring things out over waiting to be taught

Bonus points
You've played with OpenClaw, MCP, or any AI agent framework
You've deployed something to a VPS or cloud instance before
You know your way around monitoring tools (Langfuse, Grafana, etc.)
You have opinions about LLMs, tokens, and model routing
You've built a bot, a home server, a Raspberry Pi project, or anything that proves you like tinkering

You don't need to tick every box. If you're curious, resourceful, and excited about AI agents — we'd rather hear from you than not.

Why join us
You'll get production experience with AI agents that most people only read about. You'll work directly with two founders — one from Atlassian engineering, one from enterprise strategy — who'll give you real responsibility from day one, not busywork. And you'll be joining at the ground floor of a company in one of the fastest-moving spaces in tech.

To apply
Send an email to join@zerotoclaw.ai with a short note about why this interests you, your resume/LinkedIn, and ideally one example of something technical you've built or deployed (a side project, a home server, a bot, anything that shows you can ship).

About the job
At NCS Australia, we are more than a technology services company—we are a team of innovators, problem-solvers, and creators shaping the future. With a strong presence across the APAC region, we partner with government and enterprise organisations to tackle complex challenges and deliver solutions that drive real, lasting impact.

 

We are passionate about unlocking value through technology. By focusing on people, committing to quality, and challenging traditional thinking, we believe technology services can be done better. Our team brings this to life by advising, transforming, and managing technology to help our clients and communities thrive.

 

We love big ideas and bold thinkers. Bring your ambition and your creativity—together, we will turn them into extraordinary opportunities. Ready to make tomorrow with us? Explore our opportunities and join a team that is redefining what’s possible.



Job Description

The role can be based in Canberra / Brisbane / Melbourne

As a Software Engineer at NCS Australia, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining robust backend web services within complex, multi-layered architectures. This role is built for an engineer who thrives on technical variety; one day you’ll be deep in the .NET ecosystem (C#, ASP.NET, WPF, WCF) building mission-critical services, and the next you’ll be crafting intuitive frontend experiences using Angular and modern JavaScript frameworks. You will deliver solutions with a high degree of autonomy, taking full ownership of the end-to-end lifecycle—from initial design through to deployment and production monitoring in large-scale IT projects.

 

At the heart of our delivery model is a commitment to "Quality by Design." You will be a champion for Test-Driven Development (TDD) and Behavior-Driven Development (BDD), ensuring that code quality and maintainability are baked in from the first line. You won't just write features; you will design and implement sophisticated automated test suites using industry-standard tools like Selenium, Appium, and SpecFlow. By managing continuous automated testing through the Microsoft stack (TFS, MSTest), you will ensure that our solutions meet the highest standards of performance, security, and accessibility before they ever reach a user.

 

Collaboration is central to how we work. You will be an active participant in all Scrum ceremonies, working closely with Product Owners and Scrum Masters to refine user stories and define clear acceptance criteria. Beyond your individual contributions, you will uphold our "Definition of Done" through rigorous peer reviews and documentation. As a senior member of the engineering community, you will support a culture of continuous improvement, sharing your knowledge and mentoring others to elevate both the development and testing capabilities of the entire team.



Qualifications

.NET Specialist: You have extensive experience in C#, ASP.NET, WPF, and WCF, with a deep understanding of how to build and maintain web services in enterprise environments.

Full Stack & Mobile Capability: You are proficient in Angular and modern JS frameworks. Experience with Xamarin (C#) for mobile development is a distinct advantage.

Automation Architect: You are skilled in designing test frameworks using Selenium, Appium, SpecFlow, or Cucumber, and you know how to integrate them into TFS/Azure DevOps pipelines.

Architectural Mindset: You apply enterprise architecture principles and reference models to ensure your code aligns with broader business and technical strategies.

Data & Tooling Proficient: You have practical experience with SQL, XML, and package management tools like NuGet and npm.

Agile Advocate: You are experienced in the Scrum framework, comfortable with estimation, and passionate about refining user stories to ensure maximum testability.

Quality Obsessed: You don't just "do testing"—you promote TDD/BDD as a core philosophy to detect defects early and keep technical debt low.

Collaborative Mentor: You enjoy helping others grow, regularly sharing insights on development best practices and the evolution of test automation frameworks.

Willing and able to obtain a AGSV Security clearance



Additional Information

Why join us: 

NCS Australia is where you can feel at home, nurturing your talents and skills as we make tomorrow together, one day at a time. Our benefits include paid parental leave, initiatives focused on your well-being and discounted health insurance. You will also enjoy discounts on various products and services and be regularly recognised and rewarded for high performance. We are committed to your career development through our Capability Fingerprint, industry and partner training programs, special interest groups, and an AI-driven learning platform. No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

Build world‑leading software for mission‑critical solutions.

Grabba Technologies Group is an Australian leader in biometric and identity technology—and the only mobile biometric hardware manufacturer in Australia. As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a Senior Software Engineer to play a key role in delivering innovative, reliable software that outpaces the market and sets new industry standards.

About the Role

As a Senior Software Engineer at Grabba, you’ll be a senior member of our software development team, working at the intersection of architecture, delivery, and operations.

Reporting to the Engineering Manager, you’ll take a hands‑on leadership role—shaping technical direction, mitigating risk across the full software lifecycle, and ensuring our solutions perform flawlessly in production. You’ll collaborate closely with engineers, stakeholders, and customers to turn complex requirements into robust, real‑world outcomes.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Act as a senior contributor within the software engineering team, promoting technical excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement

Proactively identify and mitigate technical risks across the development lifecycle

Take accountability for production performance, incident resolution, and post‑incident improvements
Mentor and support team members, fostering best practices and professional growth
Lead code reviews, technical approvals, and design discussions to ensure quality and consistency
Work closely with internal stakeholders and customers to deliver a high‑quality, reliable solution

About You

You’re an experienced, pragmatic senior developer who thrives in environments where reliability, performance, and accountability matter.

A Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
5+ years’ experience in a senior (or equivalent) software development role

Outpace and Outperform – Swifter, smarter, sharper

Hybrid working – a balanced mix of home and office
4.5‑day work week – finish at 12:00 pm every Friday
Paid birthday leave – enjoy your birthday your way
Career growth in a dynamic, global business
Ongoing learning and development opportunities
